title: S&P 500, Nasdaq fall as tech selling resumes, Trump vows to react to downed US helicopter
summary: S&P 500 closed -0.27% at 7,385.48; Nasdaq -0.98% at 25,675.19; Dow +0.14% at 50,857.58. Tech sector fell >4% intraday before paring losses; SOX index dropped as much as 8.6% intraday. Trump said Iran shot down a US Apache helicopter patrolling Strait of Hormuz and vowed response. VIX hit highest since April 7. Rotation from momentum/tech into value. SpaceX IPO Friday ($75B raise, $1.75T valuation).

title: Stock market news for June 9, 2026
summary: S&P 500 -0.26% to 7,386.65; Nasdaq -0.97% to 25,678.82; Dow +0.17% to 50,872.11. 301 S&P 500 stocks advanced, 9/11 sectors higher except Tech (-2.9%) and Energy (-1.6%). WTI settled $88.20 (-3.4%) on ceasefire hopes. Gold $4,286.4/oz (-1.76%), lowest since Dec 2025. Existing home sales +3.2% MoM to 4.17M SAAR. GSK acquires Nuvalent for $10.6B. OpenAI confidentially filed for IPO.

title: Nasdaq, S&P 500 lead losers after Trump says U.S. 'must respond' to Iran retaliation
summary: Nasdaq fell >3% intraday after Trump said US must respond to Iran helicopter attack. S&P 500 down >2% intraday before recovering to -0.26%. Small caps held up better. NFIB Small Business Optimism fell to 95.3; uncertainty index hit 91. May CPI expected +4.2% YoY Wednesday. BTC -2% to $62,533. Strategy Inc. bought 1,550 BTC for $101M.

title: Fed Signals Shift At June Meeting With Markets Pricing In 2026 Hike
summary: Fed expected to abandon easing bias at June 16-17 FOMC (first under Chair Kevin Warsh). Markets pricing 1-2 rate hikes in 2026, cuts off the table. Gov. Waller on May 22 supported removing easing bias language. April CPI: headline 3.8%, core 2.8%, both well above 2% target. May CPI due June 10.

title: US will retaliate after Iran downs chopper: Trump
summary: Iran shot down US Army Apache helicopter patrolling Strait of Hormuz near Oman coast. Both crew safe. First loss of US military aircraft since April 8 ceasefire. US retaliatory strikes launched 5pm ET Tuesday targeting Iranian sites, described as self-defense and proportional. Iran disavowed responsibility, calling it possible human error. Ceasefire still nominally standing.

title: Crude Oil - Price, Chart, Historical Data, News
summary: WTI: $89.48/bbl (-2.01% daily, -8.80% monthly, +37.64% yearly). Brent: $92.51/bbl (-2.73% daily, -11.23% monthly, +38.35% yearly). Oil fell ~3% on Iran-Israel ceasefire agreement. Strait of Hormuz still effectively closed by dual US-Iran blockade. China crude imports at 8-year low of ~7.8M bpd. OPEC+ approved another 188k bpd hike for July.

title: Weekly Crypto Market Wrap: 9 June 2026
summary: BTC -14% weekly, held above $60k; ETH -15.8%, broke below $2,000. US spot ETF outflows: BTC $1.72B, ETH $173M. Strategy bought 1,550 BTC for $101M. Front-end IV spiked to mid-40s, 1wk ATM briefly ~60%. $5.4B leveraged positions liquidated. Positioning substantially cleaner: OI down, funding soft, 3-month basis compressed to ~3%. Market in consolidation, not panic.

title: Gold Rebounds From Two-Month Low As Iran-Israel Escalation Offsets Fed Hike Fears
summary: Gold spot $4,331.17/oz (+0.18%); silver $68.69/oz (+0.54%). Gold rebounded from two-month low of $4,313.99 on June 8. Rate-hike odds at 72% after strong May payrolls. Iran-Israel escalation triggered safe-haven bid. Physical coin/bar demand Q1 2026: 474 metric tonnes, +42% YoY, second-highest quarter on record. Central banks resumed net buying in April.

title: Stock market today: Dow rises, S&P 500, Nasdaq fall as tech rotation accelerates
summary: S&P 500 fell as much as 2.7% intraday, closed down 0.8% in afternoon trading. Markets on edge ahead of Wednesday CPI report. Broadcom's disappointing forecast on Friday triggered the tech rout that continued Tuesday.

title: Treasury Yields, Dollar Briefly Rise on Trump's Iran Retaliation Threat
summary: DXY reached 96.62 and receded to 96.48 (discrepancy with Trading Economics showing 99.73). US Treasury yields little changed in directionless trade. Dollar briefly rose on Trump retaliation threat then faded.

title: SpaceX plans to set IPO price at $135 per share, targeting record $75 billion raise
summary: SpaceX plans fixed-price IPO at $135/share to raise $75B at ~$1.75T valuation. Largest IPO ever. Demand reportedly approaching 4x oversubscription. Causing market overhang as funds reposition. Unconventional fixed-price structure seen as confidence signal.

title: Existing-Home Sales May 2026
summary: Existing-home sales +3.2% MoM in May 2026 to 4.17M SAAR (consensus 4.05M). Median price $429,300 (+1.3% YoY). Sales increased in Northeast, Midwest, South; unchanged in West. Trade deficit April: $55.9B, YTD down 49% vs year ago.
